what_it_does: This file is a living foundation document for work built with or for Tim: it records evolving understanding of Tim, his cognitive style, project laws, the Company framing, operational protocols, and built substrate, and it instructs future agents to read it before substantial work and update it additively.
observations: Declares 'Tim is the foundation' as a first law of the project.
Mandates additive, dated, layered updates and forbids silent overwrites.
Documents Tim as an autodidact founder/CEO who works 100% through AI agents and will not read code.
States there will never be other developers or human readers of code; all engineering discipline is the AI's responsibility.
Encodes a 'Company' framing with Tim as Commander/Captain/Tactician and agents as crew/staff across departments and levels.
Defines 'open-future mode' as a load-bearing writing protocol: provisional, expansion-ratio>1, no closure language.
Requires the Company to present as one entity to Tim despite internal differentiation.
Lists explicit anti-patterns that break the work, such as summarizing dense messages, treating examples as specs, forcing MVPs, and losing vocabulary.
Includes a claim-register intention: distinguish stated/inferred/verified/extended claims.
Preserves primary sources verbatim at the bottom to prevent interpretation drift.
Adds an open register at the end of each layer naming what remains unresolved.
Inventories the local AI substrate (vLLM, embeddings, Ollama, Open WebUI, models on disk).
Introduces concepts like branch+return dialogue control, hermeneutic spiral review, and modes as a Company primitive.
Includes a 'Skim Test' requiring bold claims and self-telling headers because Tim skims.
standouts: The Company is one entity and Tim is its Commander; internal complexity must not be Tim's burden.
Open-future writing mode is architectural, not stylistic: closure-form writing is structurally incompatible with success.
Tim is the sole foundation and sole human operator; generic-user assumptions are explicitly invalid.
The success function is 'the multiplier on Tim's output', not throughput or task completion.
AI-only operation makes multi-session memory and self-discipline load-bearing for survival.
conventions: Additive, dated amendments; no silent replacement.
Avoids closure language ('is', 'consists of'); uses 'includes', 'among others', 'as of [date]'.
Ends example lists with '(examples; more exist, surface as encountered)'.
Labels claims by provenance: stated by Tim, inferred, verified, extended-from-example.
Ends each layer with an explicit 'Open at this layer' register.
Uses bold text for claims and headers that tell the story alone for skimming.
Records vocabulary offered by Tim as canonical and does not substitute standard terms.
concerns: The document is very long and dense, which may itself impede the 'read whole file first' onboarding ritual.
Despite anti-closure intent, the file's authority could calcify if future agents treat its current layers as complete.
Many mechanisms (claim register format, correction propagation, one-entity synthesis, modes auto-detection) are described but not yet designed.
The provided excerpt is truncated, so the full set of layers and primary sources is not visible here.
Dates are given as 2026-05-26/27, which may be future-relative and could confuse version tracking.
notes: This is the canonical, growable foundation manifesto for a Tim-centered, AI-only Company, encoding how to read, write, remember, and build around Tim.
questions: What is the exact current project brief or first concrete build task?
How are memory entries structured and linked from this document?
How will the Company implement 'one coherent voice' across internal specialists?
What is the precise design of the correction-propagation mechanism?
How does this document relate to the parallel ~/company/foundation/system/ branch?
What are the actual departments and their responsibilities?
What is the concrete format of the claim register?
What will the Commander's bridge interface look like and when will it replace the CLI?
How are modes auto-detected and how are new modes proposed and locked?
What is the full scope of Tim's original field beyond the three examples given?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a living, growable foundation for understanding; actual content is a dense, layered constitution, onboarding ritual, cultural spec, and operational rulebook that also inventories substrate. It largely matches but carries more prescriptive architecture than a typical foundation doc.
apparent_themes: Friction elimination and multiplier on Tim's output
Anti-generic defaults and preservation of Tim's vocabulary
Modes as a universal operational primitive
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a self-extending institutional memory and cultural constitution so that successive AI sessions can operate as one Tim-fitted Company without forcing Tim to repeat himself or re-explain his frame.
novelty: It treats a specific human as an architectural primitive, formalizes expansion-over-summarization as a load-bearing protocol, and frames an AI collaboration as a company with departments, culture, levels, and a Commander rather than as a generic agent system.
contribution: What a fused system should keep: the layered, dated, additive structure; the canonical vocabulary register; the open-future writing protocol; the explicit anti-pattern list; the primary-source archive; the open registers per layer; the Commander/Company/crew conceptual model; and the progressive-revelation reading/writing protocol.
summary_for_embedding: TIM.md is a living, layered foundation document that encodes Tim's cognitive and working laws, the AI-only Company framing, canonical vocabulary, an open-future writing protocol, and anti-patterns for building systems around Tim.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to bootstrap and preserve a shared, growable understanding across AI sessions so the Company can become more Tim-fitted over time without closure or repetition.
